title: Aiko Ebihara Oral History
creator: Tessaku
description: See this link for the most complete and recent information on this record: https://repository.californiarevealed.org/node/454965 .

Oral history with Aiko Ebihara, who was born and raised in Salem, Oregon before the war. Her parents were restaurant owners and had the general support of the non-Japanese community, but could not avoid the incarceration. The family was sent to Tule Lake concentration camp but had friends that came to visit frequently. The family relocated to Cleveland, Ohio after the war. Nearly 120,000 Japanese Americans were uprooted from their West Coast homes with the signing of Executive Order 9066 in February of 1942 and sent to inland concentration camps for four to five years.
